title: viburnum smithianum
intro: Viburnum smithianum, also known as the Rose Service or Rose Burford, is a flowering plant species within the Adoxaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Viburnum smithianum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
